headline: Jewish settlers escalate West Bank terrorist attacks under the guise of war with Iran
description: Within just one week, Jewish extremists have killed five Palestinians and wounded 15. While these acts are sometimes condemned by the army or the government, in practice, Israeli authorities turn a blind eye.

headline: France calls for new funding for civil nuclear energy
description: At the second world Nuclear Energy Summit, held in Paris on Tuesday, EU Commission head Ursula von der Leyen also announced a strategy to support small modular reactor investments, backed by a €200 million guarantee.

headline: UN inquiry says Russia committed 'crimes against humanity' in deporting Ukrainian children
description: The UN's Independent International Commission of Inquiry on Ukraine said Russia had deported or transferred 'thousands' of children from occupied areas of Ukraine.

headline: Celine and Chanel take bourgeois style in a new direction at Paris Fashion Week
description: In their fall-winter 2026-2027 offerings, Michael Rider and Matthieu Blazy provide a sharp perspective on the chic wardrobe.

headline: The ghosts of the new Chilean president's family
description: José Antonio Kast, who will be sworn in on Wednesday, makes no secret of his admiration for Augusto Pinochet's regime. He is less forthcoming about his father's past as a former Wehrmacht soldier and his family's role during the atrocities committed in Paine during the 1973 coup.

One week before France's local elections, electorate leans right, poll shows
description: With environmental issues taking a back seat, security issues coming to the fore and a clear increase in support for the far-right Rassemblement National, the survey conducted by Ipsos BVA for Le Monde, Cevipof and the Jean Jaurès Foundation paints an unprecedented picture of the French political landscape.

headline: The United Arab Emirates is caught in the fog of war
description: Surprised by the scale of Iran's attacks on their territory, Emirati leaders have worked to downplay the impact in order to retain the confidence of investors and tourists, while continuing to place orders for weapons.

headline: Fashion's brand ambassadors step into an age of emancipation
description: For a long time, contracts between stars and fashion houses forbade them from appearing dressed in a rival label. Today, thanks to awards ceremonies and prestigious premieres, a new trend of increasing collaborations is emerging, shaking up the relationship between luxury and celebrities.

headline: 'War is far too sensitive an issue to be managed by artificial intelligence'

byline: Axel Ockenfels, Catherine Wolfram  and  Christian Gollier
